data.title: Man Faces Six Years in Prison After Omitting $12,302,115 in CryptoPunk NFT Sales From Tax Filing
data.description:
A Pennsylvania man could serve up to six years in prison after filing false tax returns that underreported millions of dollars of income he made from selling non-fungible tokens (NFTs). Waylon Wilcox earned most of his unreported money by hawking 97 CryptoPunk NFTs in 2021 and 2022, according to the U. S
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/04/15/man-faces-six-years-in-prison-after-omitting-12302115-in-cryptopunk-nft-sales-from-tax-filing/

data.title: Before the epic rebound of US stocks, Trump's close ally sold US bonds to buy Tesla
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Before US President Trump announced a 90-day suspension of tariffs on most countries and triggered an epic rebound in US stocks, Congresswoman Marjorie Taylor Greene sold US bonds and bought Amazon, Blackstone Group and Tesla stocks. The congresswoman has a close relationship with Trump, and the above transactions were disclosed in her financial report last week. These transactions are part of Greene's investment shift and were implemented on April 8 and April 9. Congressional Democrats have called for an investigation into whether people associated with Trump took advantage of the announcement of the tariff suspension to profit. (Jinshi)

data.title: MiCA turns 100 days: EU’s crypto landscape transformed amid tight stablecoin licensing
data.description:
16-4-2025 – A hundred days since the European Union’s ambitious Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation came into full force across its 27 member states, the cryptocurrency landscape is undergoing a profound transformation. This landmark framework, designed to harmonise rules for exchanges, stablecoin issuers, and crypto platforms, has set a high bar with stringent licensing and [. .

data.title: Google mandates MiCA compliance for crypto advertising in EU
data.description:
16-4-2025 – Google has unveiled stringent new requirements for cryptocurrency advertisements across the European Union. The tech giant’s revised policy, effective 23rd April, mandates that crypto firms must secure Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) licensing to continue their advertising campaigns. The sweeping reforms herald a new era of regulatory oversight, establishing uniform standards across the EU’s [.
source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/google-mandates-mica-compliance-for-crypto-advertising-in-eu/
